junior_vs_senior_signals.why_it_matters: Hitting a structural ceiling mid-career after a decade of optimization is a much worse outcome than choosing the market with the higher real ceiling earlier

junior_vs_senior_signals.junior_pattern: Treats the green card as a defined near-term milestone and makes major life decisions (home purchase, business plans) around an assumed timeline
junior_vs_senior_signals.senior_pattern: Looks up actual priority date and USCIS cutoff date, models realistic wait time, and makes decisions based on the probability-weighted scenario of permanent temporary status
junior_vs_senior_signals.why_it_matters: Indian nationals who discover 50–100 year backlogs mid-career have already paid a decade of immigration tax optimizing for an outcome that was never realistic

junior_vs_senior_signals.junior_pattern: Uses the home country opportunity landscape as it was when they left (often 5–10 years ago) and assumes the US remains categorically superior
junior_vs_senior_signals.senior_pattern: Actively researches current home market data — VC rankings, unicorn counts, sector competitiveness, returnee premium — and updates priors based on current conditions
junior_vs_senior_signals.why_it_matters: The home market has materially improved since 2020 in China and India; decisions based on outdated mental models systematically undervalue the return option

junior_vs_senior_signals.junior_pattern: Models financial future against FAANG-level or elite outcomes because that is what the social environment makes salient
junior_vs_senior_signals.senior_pattern: Explicitly estimates probability of achieving elite vs. median outcomes and weights the financial model accordingly before committing to the US investment
junior_vs_senior_signals.why_it_matters: The US path only generates clearly positive ROI at elite outcomes; for median outcomes the $400k–$800k investment may not beat a home country career that started earlier

junior_vs_senior_signals.junior_pattern: Compares earned income streams only, ignoring family assets, inherited property, and wealth floors available in the home country
junior_vs_senior_signals.senior_pattern: Includes family assets, property, business interests, and inheritance expectations in net worth comparisons, recognizing these as real financial variables
junior_vs_senior_signals.why_it_matters: Ignoring family wealth distorts the comparison and systematically undervalues the home country option for professionals from property or business-owning families

junior_vs_senior_signals.junior_pattern: Treats stay-vs-return as a binary, permanent, high-stakes choice that must be resolved once and is then fixed
junior_vs_senior_signals.senior_pattern: Treats it as a staged, milestone-driven, revisable strategy — defines a 3-5 year sprint toward a specific credential or financial goal, with a deliberate decision point built in
junior_vs_senior_signals.why_it_matters: The staged framing unlocks the highest-expected-value path and converts a paralyzing decision into an actionable plan; the binary framing produces either indefinite default or premature commitment

junior_vs_senior_signals.junior_pattern: Evaluates US vs. home country as the complete option set
junior_vs_senior_signals.senior_pattern: Expands the option set to include third geographies (Canada, Singapore, UAE, UK) before evaluating, asking which geography solves for both career quality and immigration stability simultaneously
junior_vs_senior_signals.why_it_matters: The best answer may exist outside the two obvious options; failing to construct the full option set means the decision is made before the analysis begins

junior_vs_senior_signals.junior_pattern: Plans around the green card they are waiting for without verifying whether it will arrive within a career-relevant timeframe
junior_vs_senior_signals.senior_pattern: Calculates actual priority date movement rates for their specific category and country of birth, then plans explicitly around what is available (EB-1A, O-1, Canada PR, return) rather than what is hoped for
junior_vs_senior_signals.why_it_matters: An entire career plan built on a 50-100+ year queue is not a plan — it is a structural trap that the senior practitioner identifies and exits early

junior_vs_senior_signals.junior_pattern: Compares today's established host-country life to today's (or yesterday's) home-country conditions
junior_vs_senior_signals.senior_pattern: Compares forward-looking trajectories — where both locations will be in 5 years — and weights the compounding growth of home markets that are on steep upward curves
junior_vs_senior_signals.why_it_matters: Present-state comparisons systematically undervalue high-growth return destinations; trajectory comparisons are what actually predict which decision will look correct in a decade

junior_vs_senior_signals.junior_pattern: Evaluates the decision primarily through salary and financial metrics; treats non-quantifiable costs (psychological cost of permanent temporariness, missed family milestones, career restriction) as secondary or ignores them
junior_vs_senior_signals.senior_pattern: Explicitly accounts for the documented psychological cost of indefinite work-visa status — the 50% trapped, 80% career-restricted finding — and treats it as a real cost in the decision model
junior_vs_senior_signals.why_it_matters: Optimizing for financial outcomes while accumulating undisclosed psychological debt produces a decision that looks right on a spreadsheet and feels wrong in practice for years

junior_vs_senior_signals.junior_pattern: Assumes they will 'figure out the visa thing' when they are ready to start a company, not realizing the structural barriers (O-1, EB-1A, NIW, or abandoning status) that this requires
junior_vs_senior_signals.senior_pattern: Maps the specific legal pathway available for founding on their current visa status against their actual credential profile, and factors this into the stay-vs-return decision years before they plan to found
junior_vs_senior_signals.why_it_matters: By the time you are ready to found and discover the barriers, you have already foreclosed the options (return with no friction, third-country base) that were available earlier

junior_vs_senior_signals.junior_pattern: Frames return as starting over, competing as a junior, or giving up hard-won progress
junior_vs_senior_signals.senior_pattern: Recognizes the returnee premium — international credentials, cross-cultural experience, global best-practice knowledge are scarce and valued — and enters home markets at senior levels with explicit equity expectations
junior_vs_senior_signals.why_it_matters: The junior framing leads to either not returning (missing the window of maximum advantage) or returning with underpriced negotiating posture; the senior framing captures the structural advantage
